defends: Inbound webhooks must authenticate the sender: verify an HMAC-SHA256 signature over the raw request body (constant-time compare) and reject anything unsigned, wrong-signed, or replayed (baseline §8 — the integrations boundary calls n8n via hooks; those hooks must prove who is calling).

live_targets.signature_scheme: n8n inbound webhooks sign with the `x-n8n-signature` header — HMAC-SHA256 of the raw body under a shared secret.

unsigned_routes.status: inbound route was UNSIGNED — needs HMAC + replay window
inbound route was UNSIGNED — needs HMAC + replay window

note: Fixed evidence manifest — what the webhook-auth check probes and how it judges is not model discretion (WORKING_METHOD §1). The check is an ACTIVE probe: it signs a payload with --secret and the configured header, confirms a correctly-signed payload is ACCEPTED, then injects an unsigned and a wrong-signature payload and confirms BOTH are REJECTED. fake_test_secret is used ONLY by the bundled mock receivers (fixtures/webhook-auth/server.mjs); the real --secret for a live n8n route is never stored here. Live n8n routes (verdict, email-report) are NOT reached from this offline self-guard — see TE-16; they must be probed against the running instance with the real shared secret.
